summary: 10 concrete items surfaced from 21 qualifying stories; strongest signals came from dev tools, Office rendering, sandboxing, and AI learning/workflow tools.

items.name: Linear
items.source_story_title: How's Linear so fast? A technical breakdown
items.summary: Product-development tracker with fast local-first sync and strong UX.
items.why_included: Commenters repeatedly praised Linear's speed, engineering, and polish.
items.evidence: The thread called Linear the best web app and bug tracker several people had used, with repeated praise for its sync engine and optimistic UI.

items.name: Silurus/ooxml
items.source_story_title: Silurus/ooxml: Pixel-faithful Office documents, rendered in the browser
items.summary: Browser-based Office document renderer with pixel-faithful output.
items.why_included: Commenters liked the rendering quality and practical browser workflow.
items.evidence: Replies said DOCX and PPTX renders were readable and impressive, and noted the separate wasm bundles for each file type.

items.name: Backrest
items.source_story_title: Backrest – a web UI and orchestrator for restic backup
items.summary: Web UI and orchestrator for restic backups.
items.why_included: A commenter said it already works well in practice for real backups.
items.evidence: One reply praised the UI for backup configuration and said the command hooks are useful with Uptime Kuma when backups fail or are missed.

items.name: Lathe
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Lathe – Use LLMs to learn a new domain, not skip past it
items.summary: LLM study tool that turns source material into structured learning plans.
items.why_included: The thread showed clear demand for a tool that teaches rather than shortcuts learning.
items.evidence: Commenters said they would try it, compared it with notebook-style learning, and discussed similar local LLM study workflows.

items.name: modernc.org/sqlite
items.source_story_title: sqlite: A CGo-free port of SQLite/SQLite3
items.why_included: The thread made a concrete case for SQLite without FFI glue.
items.evidence: Commenters pointed readers to the Go package and primary repo, then explained why avoiding cgo matters for Go codebases.

items.name: Public Domain Image Archive
items.summary: Public-domain image archive with provenance notes.
items.why_included: Commenters found the archive genuinely useful, not just pretty.
items.evidence: People praised the Infinite View, the reuse guidance, and the provenance-first approach to public-domain images.

items.name: Kyushu
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Kyushu – A self-hostable WASM sandbox for JavaScript workers
items.summary: Self-hostable WASM sandbox for JavaScript workers.
items.why_included: Commenters connected it to concrete sandbox and edge-runtime use cases.
items.evidence: Replies said it fits untrusted user code, plugin systems, and edge functions, and appreciated the standalone artifact model.

items.name: Symbolica
items.source_story_title: Symbolica 2.0: Programmable Symbols for Python and Rust
items.summary: Programmable symbolic math library for Python and Rust.
items.why_included: The thread showed real interest in the updated API and benchmarks.
items.evidence: Commenters praised the newer Rust ergonomics and asked detailed questions about algebra features, while the author replied in-thread.

items.name: Msty Claw
items.source_story_title: Anthropic, please ship an official Claude Desktop for Linux
items.summary: Cross-platform Claude-style desktop app with encrypted mobile companion.
items.why_included: It was the clearest concrete alternative surfaced in the Linux desktop thread.
items.evidence: A commenter explicitly pointed to Msty Claw as a generic multi-OS alternative to Claude Desktop.

items.name: Runner
items.source_story_title: Anthropic, please ship an official Claude Desktop for Linux
items.summary: Cowork++ desktop app with Linux support.
items.why_included: The thread surfaced it directly as a Linux-capable alternative.
items.evidence: The author posted Runner in-thread and noted that the app supports Linux.
